name: Castles and Mauriac Chalet - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park
name_original: Châteaux Forts et Chalet de Mauriac - boucle dans le Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Saint-Symphorien, located in Gironde, France, traverse a landscape characterized by the vast pine forests of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Park. The region features varied natural landscapes, including biodiverse wetlands and gentle hills, with the Leyre River offering tranquil spots. The terrain generally presents mild elevation changes, making it suitable for a range of cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Symphorien The most popular touring cycling route is Castles and Mauriac Chalet - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park and passes by historical castles. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Lagunes du Gât Mort et Domaine Gérard Lagors - loop in Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne, a moderate 30.4 miles (48.9 km) path. This route offers views of the Gât Mort lagoons and includes the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors. Local touring cyclists also love the Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail leading through the natural park and to the lake beach, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Symphorien is defined by extensive pine forests, tranquil river paths along the Leyre, and varied natural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Saint-Symphorien are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Symphorien's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Saint-Symphorien?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes tracked around Saint-Symphorien, offering nearly 3,000 kilometers of cycling opportunities. These routes cater to various skill levels, with a good selection of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Saint-Symphorien?
A: The terrain around Saint-Symphorien is characterized by the vast pine forests of the Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Park. You'll find varied natural landscapes, including biodiverse wetlands and gentle hills. The routes generally feature mild elevation changes, making them suitable for a wide range of cycling abilities.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Saint-Symphorien offers numerous easy routes perfect for beginners or family outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41537886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Path Between Two Lakes loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.7 km trail with minimal elevation, providing a pleasant ride through natural surroundings.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through the scenic Landes de Gascogne Regional Nature Park. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/662144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château de Villandraut&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy natural beauty at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens lake beach&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3916444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunes du Gât Mort&lt;/a&gt;. The Leyre River also offers tranquil spots for breaks.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Saint-Symphorien are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966625356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles and Mauriac Chalet - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 49 km circuit.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Saint-Symphorien?
A: The touring cycling routes in Saint-Symphorien are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive pine forests, tranquil river paths, and the varied natural landscapes that define the area's cycling experience.

Q: Can I find routes that include the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors?
A: Yes, the Domaine Départemental Gérard Lagors, known for its white sandy beaches, is featured on some routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961534640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunes du Gât Mort et Domaine Gérard Lagors - loop in Parc naturel régional des Landes de Gascogne&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 48.9 km route that includes this area.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Saint-Symphorien?
A: While cycling is enjoyable for much of the year, August is particularly popular for touring cycling in the Saint-Symphorien region. The pleasant weather and vibrant atmosphere make it an ideal time to explore the routes.

Q: Are there any longer touring routes for more experienced cyclists?
A: For those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39228919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bazas Cathedral – Saint-Symphorien loop from Saint-Symphorien&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate challenge over 55.1 km, allowing for a more extensive exploration of the region's landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there routes that lead to lake beaches?
A: Yes, you can find routes that take you to beautiful lake beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41.8 km trail that leads directly to the lake beach, perfect for a refreshing stop.

Q: What are the typical durations for touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Route durations vary depending on length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate 49 km route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966625356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castles and Mauriac Chalet - loop in the Landes de Gascogne Regional Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours and 15 minutes, while a 41.8 km route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7012271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostens Station – Hostens lake beach loop from Hostens&lt;/a&gt; might take about 3 hours and 40 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.
tips: Incredible ruins of the 14th century Budos castle, in the middle of the vineyards. Under restoration by Adichats association for about thirty years.

Villandraut Castle is a well-preserved 12th century medieval castle. Its construction was completed in 1312. It is one of the oldest castles still accessible in Gironde.

If you want to visit it, all the info here:
https://www.chateaudevillandraut.fr/visites-animations/visites-guid%C3%A9es-visites-libres/
Greenway in the heart of the Landes de Gascogne natural park, on Euro-velo 3, from La Brède to Langon.

House where the writer François Mauriac spent his childhood. A large chalet on the banks of the Hure river surrounded by a 10 hectare park.

The chalet now hosts writing residencies.
